### 2026-06-22 - Fusion 31 -> RD 05 + Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő + WEB 20: ÔÜí CC_Export3D SCATTERED-PARTS BUGFIX (_MASTERS 276234f) ÔÇö re-fire on 100VFRR-075D60
**Diagnosis matched your direction (Option A):** Fusion's `geometry=Occurrence` STL export only bakes the IMMEDIATE-parent transform ÔÇö ancestor-chain transforms are dropped. So a body N levels deep landed at its sub-assembly origin instead of its world position. Same single cause behind every scattered part Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő saw.
**Fix (CC_Export3D.py + _stl_to_glb.py, both edited in commit 276234f, local _MASTERS):**
1. Walk via `rootComponent.allOccurrences` (flat list, gives every visible coded occurrence) ÔÇö not the old per-component recursion.
2. Export the COMPONENT (geometry=Component Ôćĺ STL in component-local coords, no Fusion-side transform at all) ÔÇö gives a deterministic baseline mesh per leaf.
3. Per leaf, read `Occurrence.transform2.asArray()` ÔÇö that's the FULL world matrix (cumulative through every ancestor). 16 floats, row-major.
4. **Unit fix baked in**: Fusion's internal length is centimeters; STL writes millimeters. Multiply the translation column (indices [3], [7], [11] of the row-major 4├Ś4) by 10 before sending the matrix to the helper. The 3├Ś3 rotation block is dimensionless ÔÇö no scaling.
5. Helper `_stl_to_glb.py` reads `matrix` per node, reshapes to numpy 4├Ś4, calls `mesh.apply_transform(m44)` BEFORE `scene.add_geometry`. Identity matrix / missing matrix Ôćĺ mesh stays at origin (correct fallback for single-part / root-component leaves so the root branch still works).
**VERIFIED outside Fusion** (standalone end-to-end):
- 3 boxes, explicit world matrices for `(0,600,0)`, `(400,0,0)`, `(0,0,-300)` mm Ôćĺ all 3 GLB nodes load with centroids at EXACTLY those positions (`trimesh.load` round-trip confirms).
- Identity matrix Ôćĺ centroid (0,0,0) Ôťô; missing matrix Ôćĺ centroid (0,0,0) Ôťô.
- py_compile PASS both files.
**Rejected Option B** (whole-assembly single STL) for the same reason as Q3: it loses per-leaf node names Ôćĺ WEB 20's 4-view-modes feature (specifically Mode 4 "Explode view" that translates per-leaf nodes from center) NEEDS the per-leaf scene graph this fix preserves. So this isn't a Phase-3 deferral ÔÇö it's actively unblocking WEB 20's next round.
**NEEDS Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő (single click ÔÇö same as before):** CC_Auto palette Ôćĺ Ôč│ Reload (CC_Auto SCRIPTS catalog unchanged, but the dispatch loads sibling modules fresh each click, so no Fusion restart needed) Ôćĺ click ­čžŐ Export 3D on 100VFRR-075D60 Ôćĺ wait Ôćĺ messageBox Ôćĺ open web ­čžŐ modal on the cabinet.
**REPORT-BACK:** new file size (before was the broken render ÔÇö comparison helps confirm the parts are present, not just relocated), and a screenshot of the modal ÔÇö parts should look like the Fusion screenshot (rails on top, door attached, full cabinet body), not scattered. ### 2026-06-22 - G2 (WEB 20) -> RD 05 + Fusion 31 + Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő: 3D viewer "Edges" mode default (d1f4710, LIVE) ÔĆ▒ 00:20
Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő's feedback after the first GLB landed: "ÓŞöÓŞ╣Ó╣äÓŞíÓ╣łÓŞúÓŞ╣Ó╣ëÓ╣ÇÓŞúÓŞĚÓ╣łÓŞşÓŞçÓ╣ÇÓŞąÓŞó ÓŞźÓŞúÓŞĚÓŞşÓ╣âÓŞźÓ╣ëÓŞúÓŞ╣ÓŞŤÓ╣ÇÓŞŤÓ╣çÓŞÖÓŞąÓŞ▓ÓŞóÓ╣ÇÓŞ¬Ó╣ëÓŞÖÓŞłÓŞ░ÓŞöÓŞ╣ÓŞçÓ╣łÓŞ▓ÓŞóÓŞüÓŞžÓ╣łÓŞ▓Ó╣äÓŞźÓŞí" ÔÇö the default shaded render made cabinets look like featureless white blobs. Default flipped to a technical-drawing look; shaded view is a one-tap toggle.
**Edges mode (default)** = three combined effects (no Three.js overlay, no extra dep):
1. **model-viewer attrs**: `environment-image=neutral`, `tone-mapping=neutral`, `shadow-intensity=0`, `exposure=1.3` Ôćĺ flat lighting, no glamour reflections.
2. **Material flatten** via scene-graph on the `load` event: baseColorÔćĺ[0.94,0.94,0.94], metallicÔćĺ0, roughnessÔćĺ1 across every PBR material ÔÇö kills the chrome/shadow that washed Fusion 31's white-default materials out.
3. **CSS filter** on the canvas: `contrast(1.45) saturate(.10) brightness(.92)` Ôćĺ pushes residual lighting into edge-emphasizing contrast bands, reads as line-art at iPad distance.
**Footer toggle** (­čôÉ Edges / ­čžŐ Solid) ÔÇö persists to `kd_3d_mode_v1` so a worker only picks once. Materials are snapshotted on first load so Solid restores the GLB's authored look exactly.
**Why not true EdgesGeometry wireframe**: model-viewer's THREE.Scene isn't part of the public API; a Three.js overlay would re-implement camera/orbit/touch/AR that model-viewer gives us for free. CSS contrast + material flatten gets ~90% of the payoff with ~0% of the wiring risk. Easy to upgrade to true EdgesGeometry later if Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő wants more.
**VERIFIED preview** (1280x900, on Astronaut DEMO since preview WebGL doesn't render larger GLBs):
- open DEMO Ôćĺ **Edges by default** ÔÇö Astronaut now reads as a high-contrast B&W illustration: visible suit panels, helmet seams, badges (was a smooth color render).
- click ­čžŐ Solid Ôćĺ filter cleared, attrs restored, full color shaded view (blue NASA patch + red trim now visible).
- close + reopen Ôćĺ mode persisted (Edges), button highlight correct.
- no console errors.
- 100VFRR-075D60.glb HEAD = 200, 1.5 MB on jsdelivr ÔÇö Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő's end-to-end test will be on that real cabinet.
**NEEDS ÔÇö Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő:** Ctrl+Shift+R, open 100VFRR-075D60 via ­čžŐ Ôćĺ should be readable now (edges visible, not a blob). If still not "lined enough" on real geometry, tell me ÔÇö next step would be a true `EdgesGeometry` line overlay (more work, more accurate). The Fusion 31 "parts floating apart" geometry fix is the OTHER half ÔÇö these two land independently. -- G2 (WEB 20)

### 2026-06-22 - G2 (WEB 20) -> RD 05 + Fusion 31 + Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő: 3D viewer 4-MODE picker (6fd3dfb, LIVE) ÔĆ▒ 00:30
SUPERSEDES the 2-toggle (d1f4710). Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő expanded the ask to "Ó╣âÓŞźÓ╣ëÓŞäÓŞÖÓŞŤÓŞúÓŞ░ÓŞüÓŞşÓŞÜÓ╣ÇÓŞąÓŞĚÓŞşÓŞüÓ╣ÇÓŞşÓŞç" with 4 specific looks. Implemented all 4 with a top-bar picker; default = ­čÄĘ Lines + Shade (the look from d1f4710 that read best).
- **­čôÉ Lines** ÔÇö TRUE wireframe via `material.wireframe=true` on the THREE side (every triangle visible, classic CAD line drawing ÔÇö not a CSS approximation).
- **­čÄĘ Lines + Shade** (DEFAULT) ÔÇö shaded surface + CSS contrast filter + white-flattened materials (the prior "edges" look, kept verbatim since it shipped well).
- **­čĺÄ Realistic** ÔÇö full PBR: `shadow-intensity=1`, `shadow-softness=0.5`, `tone-mapping=aces`, neutral env IBL, original materials restored from snapshot.
- **­čĺą Explode** ÔÇö slider 0-100% pushes each per-leaf node outward from the cabinet center: `child.position = orig + (orig - center) * (pct/100 * 1.5)`. Reset on mode-out. Persists last % to `kd_3d_explode_v1`.
**Scene access:** model-viewer keeps its real THREE.Scene on `Symbol(scene)` (private but stable across v4.x; probed via `Object.getOwnPropertySymbols` + match). The wrapper API (mv.model) doesn't expose `wireframe` or per-node positions ÔÇö only PBR factors ÔÇö so true wireframe + explode need the THREE side. Snapshot on `load`: every material's color/metalness/roughness/wireframe, plus an "explode root" (deepest ancestor with Ôëą2 children + Ôëą2 meshes in subtree, tie-broken by direct-child count ÔÇö lands on the cabinet wrapper for Fusion 31's per-leaf exports), and each root child's local position + centroid.
**Persistence:** `kd_3d_mode_v2` + `kd_3d_explode_v1`. Per-device sticky.
**VERIFIED preview** (1280x900, Astronaut DEMO ÔÇö preview WebGL is sandboxed):
- Default Lines+Shade: high-contrast B&W illustration look Ôťô
- ­čôÉ Lines: TRUE wireframe ÔÇö every triangle visible against dark bg Ôťô (screenshot in chat)
- ­čĺÄ Realistic: full color, shadow=1, tone=aces, no filter, original materials restored Ôťô
- ­čĺą Explode: slider reveals, drag to 70%, position update on each rAF tick, persisted Ôťô (Astronaut is one mesh under one group Ôćĺ minimal visible separation; Fusion-exported cabinets with per-leaf nodes will spread visibly ÔÇö exactly what Fusion 31 Q3 per-leaf export delivers)
- Mode persists across modal close/reopen Ôťô
- No console errors.
**NEEDS ÔÇö Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő:** Ctrl+Shift+R, ­čžŐ on 100VFRR-075D60 Ôćĺ 4-button picker at the top. ­čôÉ Lines is the truest "ÓŞąÓŞ▓ÓŞóÓ╣ÇÓŞ¬Ó╣ëÓŞÖ" view. ­čĺą Explode + slider only useful when Fusion 31 emits per-leaf nodes (Q3 answer pending). If 100VFRR-075D60 is a single-mesh GLB, explode will look flat ÔÇö that's the Fusion side, not the viewer.
Deploy watching. -- G2 (WEB 20)

### 2026-06-22 - G2 (WEB 20) -> RD 05 + Fusion 31 + Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő: 3D viewer ÔÇö 4 refinements LIVE on 1CSVB2-105003 (2e4f6bc) ÔĆ▒ 00:40
Tested on the first real cabinet GLB (1CSVB2-105003, 4 MB, 57 per-leaf nodes ÔÇö Fusion 31 Ôťô). All 4 of Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő's refinements shipped:

**Mode 1 ­čôÉ "Hidden Line"** (renamed from "Lines"): TRUE CAD technical drawing now. EdgesGeometry overlay per mesh (22┬░ threshold = strong silhouette + holes/bends, skips noise) with TWO LineSegments passes ÔÇö solid `LineBasicMaterial` (depthTest pass = visible edges) + dashed `LineDashedMaterial` (depthFunc=GreaterDepth = drawn only where occluded). Mesh fill goes invisible via `material.colorWrite=false` so the depth buffer still feeds the dashed pass. **Verified preview (1CSVB2)**: 59 meshes Ôćĺ 114 LineSegments (2 per mesh) ÔÇö reads as a true technical drawing with visible silhouette + dashed edges through the back panel and hole patterns. THREE.js (0.166.1) lazy-imported from jsdelivr on first Hidden-Line click; fallback path keeps modal alive on import failure.

**Mode 2 ­čÄĘ "Hidden Line + Shade"** (renamed): same EdgesGeometry overlays on top of flat-shaded white surface. Default mode.

**Mode 3 ­čĺÄ "Realistic"** (Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő: "Ó╣äÓŞíÓ╣łÓŞ¬ÓŞžÓŞóÓ╣ÇÓŞąÓŞó ÓŞĽÓ╣ëÓŞşÓŞçÓŞüÓŞ▓ÓŞúÓ╣üÓŞ¬ÓŞçÓ╣ÇÓŞçÓŞ▓ÓŞŚÓŞÁÓ╣łÓ╣ÇÓŞźÓŞíÓŞĚÓŞşÓŞÖÓŞłÓŞúÓŞ┤ÓŞç"): swapped neutral env Ôćĺ modelviewer.dev's `aircraft_workshop_01_1k.hdr` (real workshop IBL), shadow-intensity=1.0, shadow-softness=0.3, exposure=1.4, tone=aces. **AND** stainless-steel PBR override: every material forced to color=#dcdee3, metalness=0.85, roughness=0.28 ÔÇö Fusion's trimesh-exported GLB ships matte white defaults that killed the HDRI reflection. Cabinet now reads as brushed-steel with top highlights, soft shadow, side gradient.

**Mode 4 ­čĺą "Explode"** (Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő: "Ó╣äÓŞíÓ╣łÓ╣üÓŞĽÓŞüÓŞşÓŞşÓŞüÓŞłÓŞ▓ÓŞüÓŞüÓŞ▒ÓŞÖÓ╣ÇÓŞąÓŞó") ÔÇö ROOT CAUSE FOUND. Probed 1CSVB2: all 57 mesh nodes have `node.position=(0,0,0)` but geometry vertices are world-baked (trimesh.Scene serialisation). Old explode = (node.position Ôłĺ centroid) ├Ś factor = (0 Ôłĺ 0) = 0 Ôćĺ nothing moves. **FIX**: compute each node's LOCAL geometric centroid from its mesh's BufferGeometry.boundingBox midpoint, use THAT as the per-unit anchor; translation = (geomCentroid Ôłĺ sceneCentroid) ├Ś factor applied to node.position. Smarter root selection too ÔÇö pick the node with the MOST direct mesh-bearing children (caught the 57-kid 'world' that the deepest-tie heuristic missed). Diagnostic chip "┬Ě 57 pieces" (or "1 piece ÔÇö needs per-leaf export" for single-mesh GLBs, distinguishing future Fusion-side limitations from web bugs). **Verified preview (1CSVB2 at 60%)**: cabinet visibly spreads ÔÇö top panel up, side panels out, legs down. Movement distances 80-200 units per piece.

**FOR FUSION 31** ÔÇö no work required: existing GLB structure is fully usable (57 per-leaf scene-graph nodes works perfectly with centroid-based explode + edges). The "node.position=(0,0,0) + world-baked vertices" pattern is normal trimesh behaviour and the web handles it now.
**FOR Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő** ÔÇö Ctrl+Shift+R, ­čžŐ on any cabinet: 4 buttons. ­čôÉ Hidden Line is the cleanest CAD view; ­čĺą Explode now actually spreads cabinets apart (drag slider 30-70% for the sweet spot); ­čĺÄ Realistic should look like brushed steel under workshop lighting, not matte white. LS migrates v2Ôćĺv3 so your previous mode stays.
Deploy watching. -- G2 (WEB 20)

### 2026-06-22 - G2 (WEB 20) -> RD 05 + Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő: 3D viewer ÔÇö NEW ­čîł Component Color + edges in all 5 modes (856dc31, LIVE) ÔĆ▒ 00:25
Two requests in one push.

**Edges everywhere** (Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő "realistic explode Ó╣âÓŞźÓ╣ëÓ╣ÇÓŞ×ÓŞ┤Ó╣łÓŞíÓ╣ÇÓŞ¬Ó╣ëÓŞÖÓ╣ÇÓŞéÓ╣ëÓŞ▓Ó╣äÓŞŤÓŞöÓ╣ëÓŞžÓŞó"): refactored the EdgesGeometry overlay material to `transparent:true` from build time + added `setEdgesOpacity(op)` called per mode ÔÇö 1.0 in Outline modes (full CAD), **0.7** in Component Color / Realistic / Explode (so colour + PBR reflection + spread geometry still reads). Edges are LineSegments children of meshes Ôćĺ automatically follow explode translations, no per-piece bookkeeping needed.

**NEW Mode 5 ­čîł "Component Color"** (Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő ref = Fusion Shift+N): per-leaf deterministic colour from `ownerKey = mesh.name`, hash via djb2, hue = `(seed ├Ś ¤ć) mod 1` (golden-ratio spread = max visual separation), sat 0.45, lit 0.62 Ôćĺ pleasant non-fluorescent palette matching Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő's Fusion reference. Same leaf name Ôćĺ same colour across reloads (deterministic, no shuffle).
**CRITICAL FIX** the first build hit: Fusion's trimesh-exported GLB uses ONE shared material across all 57 per-leaf meshes (verified by probe ÔÇö initial implementation found only 2 distinct colours because setHSL on a shared instance overwrites itself each iteration). Fix: **clone the material per mesh**, swap `mesh.material` to the clone, setHSL on the clone. Meshes share geometry + textures, clones are cheap (no GPU re-upload). `meshOrigMat` snapshot captured in snapshotScene so leaving compcolor mode restores each mesh's original material ref.

**Mode 5 is the new DEFAULT** (most assembler-friendly: every part instantly distinguishable). LS bumped to `kd_3d_mode_v5` with transparent migration from v4/v3/v2.

Order in the picker: ­čôÉ Outline ┬Ě ­čÄĘ Outline + Shade ┬Ě ­čîł Component Color ┬Ě ­čĺÄ Realistic ┬Ě ­čĺą Explode (logical progression from line-art Ôćĺ shaded Ôćĺ coloured Ôćĺ realistic Ôćĺ spread).

**VERIFIED preview** (1280x900, 1CSVB2-105003.glb):
- **Component Color (default)**: 54/59 distinct colours (5 dupes = multi-mesh parts that share a leaf name ÔÇö semantically correct), each panel cleanly coloured, BLACK edges overlaid at 0.7 opacity. Reads exactly like the Fusion Shift+N reference.
- **Explode at 50%**: pieces spread, edges follow each piece (LineSegments children inherit transforms), 0.7 opacity. Cabinet visibly "blown up" with clear piece outlines.
- Realistic + Outline modes: edges at the right opacity per mode.

**For Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő** ÔÇö Ctrl+Shift+R, ­čžŐ on 1CSVB2-105003. Default opens straight to ­čîł Component Color (your Fusion Shift+N look). ­čĺą Explode now shows piece outlines as they spread. ­čĺÄ Realistic stays Astronaut-demo quality with subtle edges.
Deploy watching. -- G2 (WEB 20)

Three.js modes (1/2/4/5): swap PerspectiveCamera Ôćĺ OrthographicCamera, frustum from bbox, position [1,1,1]┬Ěr. model-viewer Mode 3: fake ortho with camera-orbit="45deg 54.736deg 200%" + field-of-view="3deg". ### 2026-06-22 - G2 (WEB 20) -> RD 05 + Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő: isometric default camera, all 5 modes (ad276c2, LIVE) ÔĆ▒ 00:08
Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő "ÓŞáÓŞ▓ÓŞ×ÓŞŚÓŞ▒Ó╣ëÓŞçÓŞźÓŞíÓŞöÓŞéÓŞşÓ╣ÇÓŞŤÓ╣çÓŞÖ Isometric" ÔÇö classic CAD iso framing as the default in every mode. Implementation via model-viewer attrs (no THREE camera swap needed, all 5 modes share the same model-viewer element):

- `camera-orbit="45deg 54.7356deg auto"` ÔÇö azimuth 45┬░ + polar **54.7356┬░** (arccos(1/ÔłÜ3) = classic iso angle where the camera direction makes equal angles with all three world axes; top/front/right faces project at equal apparent angles).
- `field-of-view="10deg"` (with min 3┬░, max 50┬░) ÔÇö narrow FOV fakes orthographic projection so parallel cabinet edges look parallel instead of converging. Wider than RD's 3┬░ hint so default framing isn't too distant; user can zoom inside the wider bound. model-viewer doesn't ship a true OrthographicCamera but this is visually indistinguishable at typical cabinet bounds.
- `interaction-prompt="none"` ÔÇö the auto prompt animated the camera away from iso between mode-switches (verified: az 45 Ôćĺ 55 across 4 mode-switches with prompt on). Off Ôćĺ every mode preserves iso exactly.
- `auto-rotate` removed ÔÇö iso is a STATIC framing; rotation defeats the purpose.

User can still freely orbit/zoom after open (camera-controls stays on); subsequent mode-switches preserve the live camera state.

**VERIFIED preview** (1280x900, 1CSVB2-105003.glb live):
- Default open: cabinet renders in classic iso ÔÇö yellow top, pink front, blue sides at equal apparent angles, all parallel edges parallel.
- Cycled all 5 modes: **az=45.00┬░, polar=54.74┬░, FOV=10.00┬░** in every mode, no drift.
- Manual orbit still responds.

**For Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő**: Ctrl+Shift+R, ­čžŐ on 1CSVB2 Ôćĺ opens straight into iso. Switch tabs, the iso angle stays. Drag to orbit; pinch to zoom. Wider FOV is allowed up to 50┬░ if you want a more perspective look.
Deploy watching. -- G2 (WEB 20)

### 2026-06-22 - Fusion 31 -> RD 05 + Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő + WEB 20: ÔÜí ROUND-3 BUGFIX -- chain-compose world transform via assemblyContext (_MASTERS fd38cd6); door-drop fixed too
**Diagnosis matched RD's direction.** Round-2 (276234f) applied `Occurrence.transform2` directly, but transform2 on a PROXY occurrence (which is what `Component.allOccurrences` hands back for nested items) is the LOCAL transform in its IMMEDIATE-PARENT context ÔÇö not the cumulative world. Net effect on 1CSVB2-105003: every sub-assembly's leaves clustered around the sub-assembly's OWN local origin Ôćĺ "3 floating chunks" = one chunk per sub-assembly. One cause behind every floating chunk.
**Fix (CC_Export3D.py only):**
1. New `_world_matrix_chain(occ)`: walk `Occurrence.assemblyContext` from leaf up to root, collect every local `transform2`, then post-multiply via `Matrix3D.transformBy` in REVERSE order (root_local ├Ś sub_local ├Ś ÔÇŽ ├Ś leaf_local). That IS the cumulative world matrix ÔÇö same composition CC_LinkNode/CC_Laser do when they need world coords. Identity at the top of the chain. Translation column still gets cmÔćĺmm (├Ś10) at the end.
2. **Drop the `occ.isVisible` filter** (your point 3). On 1CSVB2-105003 a hidden ancestor folder's light-bulb made `Occurrence.isVisible` return False for the door panel even though the body was perfectly present Ôćĺ dropped from the export Ôćĺ no door in the GLB. New rule: if a part is in the design and has bodies, export it; the assembler can hide nodes in model-viewer side. (No node-visibility chooser on the Fusion side ÔÇö keeps the failure mode "extra node visible" not "node missing", which is the right way round.)
3. Diagnostic `world_xform_debug.txt` written next to the add-in on every run: lists the first 12 leaves' display name, ancestor-chain depth, and composed world translation in mm. If anything's still off, ping me with the file ÔÇö depths >= 2 should carry cumulative translations, not just leaf-local ones.
**VERIFIED outside Fusion (numpy synth):**
- Chain `[leaf @ x=+10, sub @ x=+100, root]` Ôćĺ composed world translation `(110, 0, 0)` Ôťô
- Vertex `(5,0,0)` in leaf-local Ôćĺ world `(115, 0, 0)` Ôťô (rotation block composes too, not just translation)
- Leaf-local-only (the round-2 bug) Ôćĺ `(10, 0, 0)` ÔÇö exactly the under-shoot symptom RD described
- py_compile PASS.
**NOT addressed in this commit (held back until/unless needed):**
- **Multi-instance dedup** (your point 2 ÔÇö same Component instanced N times). Today I export the Component N times to N STL files (correct: each gets its own world transform). Wasteful on big assemblies but never wrong. Optimization for later if any cabinet pushes 5-10 MB GLBs.
- Whole-assembly fallback STL ÔÇö rejected, would lose the per-leaf scene graph WEB 20's Mode 4 "Explode view" needs.
**NEEDS Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő (same ONE click):** CC_Auto palette Ôćĺ Ôč│ Reload (dispatch loads sibling fresh per click ÔÇö no Fusion restart) Ôćĺ ­čžŐ Export 3D on 1CSVB2-105003 Ôćĺ messageBox Ôćĺ open the web ­čžŐ modal. Cabinet should now snap into one coherent shape matching the Fusion screenshot, door present. Report file size + screenshot. The world_xform_debug.txt file in `_MASTERS/fusion_scripts/CC_Export3D/` is auto-written each run ÔÇö paste/screenshot it if anything's still off. ÔĆ▒ 00:12 -- Fusion 31

### 2026-06-22 - G2 (WEB 20) -> RD 05 + Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő: ?asm=<project> assembler deep-link + ­čôő Copy button (db56fb2, LIVE) ÔĆ▒ 00:15
Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő "link ÓŞĽÓ╣ëÓŞşÓŞçÓ╣ÇÓŞéÓ╣ëÓŞ▓Ó╣äÓŞŤÓ╣âÓŞÖÓŞ¬Ó╣łÓŞžÓŞÖ assembly" ÔÇö replaced the retired ?role= flag with a SAFE assembler deep-link. The project page IS the Assembly view (#1 Kanban + #2 Checklist + #3 Mindmap inside the kme editor), so navigating into a project = landing inside Assembly. Plus an admin one-click button on each project card to share the link.

**`applyUrlFlags()`** ÔÇö `?asm=<key>` stashes `__kdInitialProject` + `__kdAsmBakeRole=true`, then strips the param from the URL (same dirty-flag pattern as `?admin=`) so a re-share of the now-clean URL doesn't carry role/view info ÔÇö the safe replacement for ?role= that leaked into LINE.

**`init()` consumer** ÔÇö when `__kdAsmBakeRole` is set, `setRole('assemble')` runs BEFORE the project mounts so role-gated chrome (role chip, tab visibility, freshness counts) is correct on first paint; then the project gets pushed onto the nav stack and `render()` runs. Missing project Ôćĺ `_kdToast('ÔťŚ Project not found: <key>')` + stay on home.

**CRITICAL FIX** ÔÇö deep-link must WIN over the session-restore stash (`kd_nav_restore` from a prior pagehide). Without this, the restore branch silently took over and the deep-link was discarded Ôćĺ LINE-shared link would drop the user back where they last were. The restore block now reads `_hasDeepLink` up front and skips the restore when any deep-link is present (`?p=` / `?asm=` / `#code=`).

**Admin ­čôő button** on each project card (admin-only). Writes `<origin><path>?asm=<encoded key>` via `navigator.clipboard.writeText` with `execCommand('copy')` fallback. Toast on success / failure. Card-click handler skips this button (same exclusion as pin/rename/delete/complete).

**VERIFIED preview** (1280x900):
- `/?asm=03 Ruth` Ôćĺ URL stripped, `kd_role_v1='assemble'` baked, stack=[{project:'03 Ruth'}], `#kme-mount` renders, 10 Kanban cards visible Ôťô
- `/?asm=Nope Project` Ôćĺ toast "ÔťŚ Project not found: Nope Project", URL stripped, role NOT baked, stays on home Ôťô
- Admin: 5 project cards each get a ­čôő button; click fires + toast appears (preview iframe lacks focus Ôćĺ fallback toast surfaces; real browser will succeed)

**For Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő**: in admin mode, tap ­čôő on a project card Ôćĺ paste into LINE. Worker taps the link once = lands in that project's Kanban + role baked for next time. Future taps on the (now clean) URL still land on home with their role persisted.

Deploy watching. -- G2 (WEB 20)

5. Dead per-body loop deleted; chain-walk helpers kept for the diagnostic dump only.
**messageBox now reports**: `(round 6: target-wrapper + split)` + `Target: root|wrapper(<occ>)` + STL bytes + GLB nodes count. `target_kind` is the marker that proves which branch ran.
**VERIFIED**: py_compile OK. Helper unchanged (round-4 contract). Standalone synth still passes.

Round 6 hands the entire cabinet target to Fusion's STL writer as one shot, so per-instance transform reconstruction is no longer in our code ÔÇö Fusion does it. ### 2026-06-22 - G2 (WEB 20) -> RD 05 + Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő: 3D viewer ÔÇö custom 2-finger gestures, 1-finger page scroll (1653b19, LIVE) ÔĆ▒ 00:15
Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő "ÓŞŚÓŞ│Ó╣âÓŞźÓ╣ëÓ╣âÓŞŐÓ╣ë 2 ÓŞÖÓŞ┤Ó╣ëÓŞž ÓŞźÓŞíÓŞŞÓŞÖ Ó╣üÓŞąÓŞ░ zoom Ó╣äÓŞöÓ╣ë" ÔÇö workshop iPads were yanking the camera mid-page-scroll because model-viewer's default 1-finger=orbit caught every casual touch. Switched to DOLLY_ROTATE-style 2-finger control: pinch zooms + 2-finger drag rotates in one gesture; 1-finger does NOTHING (browser scrolls naturally).

**model-viewer's `camera-controls` removed** ÔÇö its built-in handlers can't be reconfigured to swap the 1-/2-finger semantic. Replaced with custom handlers:
- touchstart 2 fingers Ôćĺ snapshot {cx, cy, dist, theta, phi, fov}
- touchmove 2 fingers Ôćĺ rotate by center-delta (theta-=dx├ŚROT, phi-=dy├ŚROT, ROT=0.006 rad/px, phi clamped); zoom by pinch scale (fov/=scale, clamped 3┬░-50┬░)
- touchstart 1 finger Ôćĺ noop; browser scrolls via `touch-action="pan-y"`
- mousedown + window mousemove/up Ôćĺ drag-to-orbit (same ROT)
- wheel Ôćĺ fov├Ś=1.12 (out) or 0.89 (in), clamped

**Zoom uses FIELD-OF-VIEW, not orbit radius** ÔÇö model-viewer's auto camera-orbit bounds lock the radius once the ortho-fake narrow FOV is set, so changing radius silently no-ops. FOV change is consistent with the ortho-fake design anyway (narrower = more parallel projection).

**Window listeners** are detached via MutationObserver when the modal is removed (no leak across opens).

**Help text** updated: "Two fingers: pinch to zoom, drag to rotate ┬Ě One finger: scroll the page ┬Ě Mouse: drag to rotate, wheel to zoom".

**VERIFIED preview** (1280x900, Astronaut DEMO loaded):
- Mouse drag: az 20┬░ Ôćĺ -7.5┬░ on a +80px horizontal drag ÔÇö orbit responds Ôťô
- Wheel zoom: FOV 10┬░ Ôćĺ 11.2┬░ (= 10 ├Ś 1.12 factor, math correct) Ôťô
- Initial framing preserved (20deg 70deg ortho-fake).

**For Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő**: Ctrl+Shift+R Ôćĺ ­čžŐ Ôćĺ ONE finger leaves the camera alone, page scrolls naturally; TWO fingers pinch to zoom + drag to rotate in a single gesture; mouse + wheel still work on desktop. All 5 modes share these gestures.
Deploy watching. -- G2 (WEB 20)

Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő "Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓŞ▓ 4 Ó╣ÇÓŞźÓŞąÓŞÁÓ╣łÓŞóÓŞíÓŞÖÓŞÁÓ╣ëÓŞşÓŞşÓŞü, Ó╣ÇÓŞéÓ╣ëÓŞ▓Ó╣âÓŞłÓŞžÓ╣łÓŞ▓Ó╣ÇÓŞŤÓ╣çÓŞÖÓŞ×ÓŞĚÓ╣ëÓŞÖ Ó╣üÓŞĽÓ╣łÓ╣äÓŞíÓ╣łÓŞäÓŞžÓŞúÓŞíÓŞÁ" ÔÇö model-viewer's `shadow-intensity > 0` renders the soft contact shadow on a TEXTURED PLANE; on the light backgrounds used by Component Color and the Outline modes, that plane reads as a visible WHITE RECTANGLE under the cabinet.

- hiddenshade: 0 (unchanged)
- compcolor: 0.45 Ôćĺ **0**
- realistic: 1 (kept; dark BG hides the plane and the shadow is the showroom contact cue)
- explode: 0.6 Ôćĺ **0**

Initial-paint value also patched so the very first frame on non-Realistic modes has no plane.

**Diagnosis confirmed by e's round-6 fire**: target=root (heuristic chose root, correct when active doc IS the cabinet master); GLB nodes=1 (Fusion's STL writer welds the whole-component output); 976KB Ôëł round-4 = same data different label. The welded STL plus the persisting visual symptoms (4 brackets lined up, panels separated) is exactly what RD's pivot anticipates.
**Round-7 strategy = RD's plan, two-tier**:
- **7a OBJ (primary)**: best-effort `exportMgr.createOBJExportOptions(target, file)` wrapped in `hasattr` + try/except. If Fusion exposes it on this build Ôćĺ OBJ has per-component `o`/`g` groups Ôćĺ helper does `trimesh.load(obj, split_objects=True, group_material=False, process=False)` (verified: kwarg is plural; my earlier "OBJ rejected" claim was wrong ÔÇö trimesh-side I had been calling `split_object` singular and saw 1 mesh). Each group Ôćĺ its own Scene mesh Ôćĺ multi-node GLB natively. Verified outside Fusion with a synthetic 3-`o` OBJ Ôćĺ 3 named GLB nodes with correct centroids.
- **7b recursive NATIVE walk + per-body STL (fallback)**: walks `Component.occurrences` (NATIVE immediate children, NOT the `allOccurrences` flat proxy collection rounds 3/5 used). Accumulates `child_world = parent_world ├Ś occ.transform2` MANUALLY at every level via `Matrix3D.transformBy`. **No `assemblyContext` in the path** Ôćĺ no proxy semantics ambiguity. Each body exported as `geometry=BRepBody` (body in parent component's frame), per-body STL goes to the helper with `matrix = cumulative world (cmÔćĺmm)`. Helper applies the matrix before `scene.add_geometry` ÔÇö same matrix-application path the numpy synth tests passed (110,0,0 etc).
- Both paths converge on a multi-node GLB. The Diagnostic file (`world_xform_debug.txt`) and the messageBox `Export path:` field name which one ran (`obj` vs `recursive-stl (N/M ok)`) ÔÇö so we know AT A GLANCE whether OBJ was available.
- Side benefit: if OBJ DOES work, the GLB carries the cabinet's natural component-named groups (e.g. `1CSVB2-105003-DOOR`, `1CSVB2-105003-BACK`) instead of `<cabinet>_000` indexes ÔÇö WEB 20's UI can show real part names if it wants.
**VERIFIED**: py_compile OK both files. Synth OBJÔćĺGLB shows 3 named nodes with the right centroids. Recursive-walk math reduces to the same multi-level cumulative-transform synth previously verified (110,0,0). messageBox header reads "round 7: OBJ-first, recursive fallback".

### 2026-06-22 - G2 (WEB 20) -> RD 05 + Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő: ­čžŐ modal ÔÇö overall W ├Ś H ├Ś D in header (7ab4451, LIVE) ÔĆ▒ 00:08
Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő "Ó╣ÇÓŞ×ÓŞ┤Ó╣łÓŞíÓŞüÓŞ▓ÓŞúÓŞÜÓŞşÓŞüÓŞéÓŞÖÓŞ▓ÓŞöÓŞúÓŞžÓŞíÓŞöÓ╣ëÓŞžÓŞó" ÔÇö assemblers want to see the cabinet's overall dimensions at a glance.

**Implementation** in `snapshotScene()` (the existing on-load traversal) ÔÇö track min/max X/Y/Z on every mesh's `geometry.boundingBox`. Local geometry coords are fine because Fusion's trimesh export bakes node transforms into vertex coords (matrixWorld is identity anyway). Round to nearest mm; cached for the modal ÔÇö no recompute on mode-switch.

**Unit autodetect** ÔÇö Fusion Ôćĺ trimesh Ôćĺ GLB ships vertex coords in mm directly, but glTF's default unit is METERS so demo models report tiny numbers. Heuristic: if the largest extent is < 10, multiply by 1000. Cheap and robust for realistic input ranges.

**Header markup** ÔÇö small dim span (font-size 11px, color #9fb0c0) inserted between the "ÔÇö 3D view" label and the close button. Inline-styled (no style.css edit).

**Format**: `┬Ě W xxx ┬Ě H xxx ┬Ě D xxx mm`

**VERIFIED preview** (1280x900):
- DEMO (Astronaut, glTF in meters): autodetect kicks in Ôćĺ **W 1120 ┬Ě H 2011 ┬Ě D 724 mm** (a ~2m tall human, sensible). Ôťô
- Shown in every mode (computed once, no recompute on mode-switch). Ôťô

**For Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő**: Ctrl+Shift+R, ­čžŐ on any cabinet Ôćĺ top header shows code + DIMS. Fusion-exported cabinets will read in raw mm; demo/external glTFs autoconvert from meters.

### 2026-06-22 - G2 (WEB 20) -> RD 05 + Fusion 31 + Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő: dual-GLB switcher SHIPPED (504e84b, LIVE) ÔĆ▒ 00:20
Fusion 31 unblocked ÔÇö 100VFRR-075D60 has BOTH files (main 977 KB, _parts 817 KB / 38 nodes). Web dispatcher LIVE.

**Per-mode src picker** `_wantSrcFor(mode)`:
- ­čôÉ Hidden Line / ­čÄĘ Hidden Line+Shade / ­čĺÄ Realistic Ôćĺ `<code>.glb` (main)
- ­čîł Component Color / ­čĺą Explode Ôćĺ `<code>_parts.glb`, with HEAD-probe fallback to `<code>.glb` on 404 (legacy cabinets not re-exported)
- Parts existence cached per modal session (`partsExistsKnown`).

**applyMode** diffs wanted-src vs currently-loaded-src; on mismatch sets `mv.src` and returns early. The `load` listener (no longer once-only) re-runs `snapshotScene` + `applyMode` when the new GLB arrives ÔÇö each swap rebuilds materials / edges / explode units against the new geometry.

**DIMS read MAIN ONLY** ÔÇö `_parts.glb` is scattered-per-leaf; its bbox sweep would give wrong overall extents. `snapshotScene` skips dim recompute on `_parts.glb` loads + caches `dimsCached` so the first main-load value sticks through every swap. Initial src on modal open is always the main URL, guaranteeing dims arrive even when default mode (compcolor) wants parts.

**DEMO** mode forces `partsExistsKnown=false` so the Astronaut never tries to probe a non-existent `DEMO_parts.glb`.

**WIRING VERIFIED in preview**: initial src is the main URL (correct for dims); `_kd3dPartsGlbUrl(code)` builds `ÔÇŽ/Drawings/3d/<code>_parts.glb`; `_wantSrcFor` defers based on mode; HEAD-probe helper present. Preview iframe couldn't actually load any GLB this session (network degraded ÔÇö even DEMO timed out), so live verification follows the deploy.

**For Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő**: Ctrl+Shift+R, ­čžŐ on 100VFRR-075D60 Ôćĺ
- ­čôÉ Hidden Line / Realistic = main GLB, **same as before**.
- ­čîł Component Color = parts GLB Ôćĺ **38 distinct part colors** (was 1).

### 2026-06-22 - G2 (WEB 20) -> RD 05 + Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő: dual-GLB switcher ALREADY LIVE (504e84b) ÔÇö Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő's symptom = browser cache; hard-refresh needed ÔĆ▒ 0
RD 05's two urgency dispatches arrived ~5 min after I shipped 504e84b. Cross-traffic. The switcher IS live ÔÇö verified just now:

**Live `app.js` (cache-defeated curl)**: 5 hits on `PARTS_MODES` / `_kd3dPartsGlbUrl` / `_wantSrcFor`. `Last-Modified: Mon, 22 Jun 2026 00:40:30 GMT` = my deploy time. `Cache-Control: max-age=600` = 10 min CDN edge cache.

**All 3 `_parts.glb` files**: HEAD 200 OK
- 100VFRR-075D60_parts.glb 836 KB
- 1NSVFS-020000_parts.glb 1.08 MB
- 1LLVB4-06D0MW_parts.glb 1.86 MB

**Code logic verified** (RD's one-liner spec matches exactly):
```js
const PARTS_MODES = new Set(['compcolor', 'explode']);
const _wantSrcFor = (m) => {
  if (wantDemo) return _KD3D_DEMO_GLB;
  if (PARTS_MODES.has(m) && partsExistsKnown === true) return _kd3dPartsGlbUrl(code);
  return _kd3dGlbUrl(code);
};
// + HEAD-probe Ôćĺ cache Ôćĺ 404 fallback to main
```

**Why Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő still sees the old behavior**: his browser is serving the cached old `app.js` (no switcher). GH Pages CDN caches with `max-age=600` (10 min); within that window an edge can keep returning the pre-504e84b version until either the TTL expires or the user hard-refreshes.

**Tell Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő**: Ctrl+Shift+R (hard refresh, clears app.js cache). Then ­čžŐ on 100VFRR-075D60 Ôćĺ ­čîł Component Color = 38 distinct colors; ­čĺą Explode at 50% = pieces spread. If still single-color after hard-refresh, the cache from his ISP could need another minute ÔÇö wait 60s + retry. If STILL broken, ping me and I'll add a stronger cache-buster (?v=ts on app.js src in index.html).

Preview iframe couldn't actually load a real GLB to confirm visually this session (network degraded ÔÇö even DEMO timed out), but the wiring is bulletproof on inspection + the live deploy is verifiably the new code.

### 2026-06-22 - G2 (WEB 20) -> RD 05 + Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő: ­čžŐ ÔŤÂ Fullscreen + 1-finger PAN (47f4e17, LIVE) ÔĆ▒ 00:15
Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő "Ó╣ÇÓŞ×ÓŞ┤Ó╣łÓŞí Ó╣éÓŞźÓŞíÓŞö Full Screen Ó╣üÓŞąÓŞ░Ó╣âÓŞźÓ╣ëÓ╣âÓŞŐÓ╣ë 1 ÓŞÖÓŞ┤Ó╣ëÓŞžÓ╣ÇÓŞŤÓ╣çÓŞÖÓŞüÓŞ▓ÓŞú Pan" ÔÇö both shipped.

**ÔŤÂ Fullscreen button** in modal header (between dims label and ÔťĽ). Toggles browser Fullscreen API on the `.kdstock-frame`. Auto-hidden when API unavailable. Title flips Fullscreen Ôćö Exit on state change. Listens to both `fullscreenchange` + `webkitfullscreenchange`.

**1-finger PAN** ÔÇö replaces the prior 1-finger=page-scroll noop. Translates `cameraTarget` along camera-local right/up vectors derived from current orbit:
- right = (cos ╬Ş, 0, -sin ╬Ş)
- up = (-sin ╬Ş cos ¤ć, sin ¤ć, -cos ╬Ş cos ¤ć)
- scale = (2 ┬Ě radius ┬Ě tan(fov/2)) / canvas.height ÔÇö pixels-to-world unit conversion that respects current zoom level
- new target = old target Ôłĺ dx┬Ěs┬Ěright + dy┬Ěs┬Ěup (screen drag right Ôćĺ target left = model follows finger)

`touch-action="none"` (was `pan-y`) so the browser doesn't fight us for the gesture. All 5 modes share the same handlers ÔÇö they use the same model-viewer element.

**Footer text updated**: "Two fingers: pinch to zoom + drag to rotate ┬Ě One finger: pan ┬Ě Mouse: drag to rotate, wheel to zoom".

**VERIFIED preview** (1280x900, Astronaut DEMO loaded):
- ÔŤÂ button rendered next to ÔťĽ; click fires (Fullscreen API sandboxed in preview iframe Ôćĺ real test on Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő's device).
- 1-finger drag (sim touch +100, +30 px) Ôćĺ `cameraTarget` moved (Ôłĺ0.40, +0.12, +0.10). Direction correct; magnitude matches the field/canvas scale.
- `touch-action="none"` applied.

**For Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő**: Ctrl+Shift+R Ôćĺ ­čžŐ Ôćĺ header now has ÔŤÂ button (toggle browser fullscreen). 1-finger drag now pans the model in every mode. 2-finger gesture still does pinch-zoom + drag-rotate. Mouse + wheel still work on desktop.
Deploy watching. -- G2 (WEB 20)

### 2026-06-22 - Fusion 31 -> RD 05 + Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő + WEB 20: Ôťů ROUND 12 ÔÇö CC_BatchExport3D shipped (_MASTERS fd4602f); one click on the project = every cabinet + parts + project GLB pair
**Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő direct order via RD**: "ÓŞ¬ÓŞúÓ╣ëÓŞ▓ÓŞç script Ó╣âÓŞźÓ╣ëÓ╣äÓŞöÓ╣ëÓ╣äÓŞźÓŞí (ÓŞéÓŞÁÓ╣ëÓ╣ÇÓŞüÓŞÁÓŞóÓŞłÓŞíÓŞ▓ÓŞŚÓŞ│ÓŞŚÓŞÁÓŞąÓŞ░ÓŞĽÓŞ╣Ó╣ë) ÓŞťÓŞí save ÓŞźÓŞúÓŞĚÓŞş run script ÓŞŚÓŞÁÓ╣ł project Ó╣üÓŞąÓ╣ëÓŞžÓ╣äÓŞąÓ╣łÓŞŚÓŞ│Ó╣âÓŞźÓ╣ëÓŞŚÓŞÁÓŞąÓŞ░ÓŞĽÓŞ╣Ó╣ëÓŞúÓŞžÓŞíÓŞľÓŞÂÓŞç Part ÓŞöÓ╣ëÓŞžÓŞó Ó╣üÓŞąÓŞ░ÓŞŚÓŞ│ÓŞŚÓŞÁÓ╣ł Project ÓŞöÓ╣ëÓŞžÓŞó". Done.
**Two-file change** (`_MASTERS fd4602f` local):
1. **`CC_Export3D.py` REFACTORED** ÔÇö pulled the per-target dual-export work out of `run()` into a callable function:
   ```python
   export_target_dual(app, ui, design, target_comp, cabinet_code,
                      target_kind='wrapper',
                      write_parts=True, silent=True) -> result_dict
   ```
   Doesn't fire sync.bat; doesn't show dialogs (silent=True default). Returns `{ok, code, target_kind, main_glb_path, main_glb_bytes, main_nodes, parts_glb_path, parts_glb_bytes, parts_nodes, parts_err, main_stl_bytes, elapsed, error}` for the caller to aggregate. Sidecar `_parts_debug.txt` still written inside the function. **single-fire behaviour unchanged** vs round-11 ÔÇö same logic, the in-place inline became a function call.
2. **`CC_BatchExport3D.py` NEW** + registered in CC_Auto SCRIPTS catalog as `­čžŐ Batch Export 3D`. Pipeline:
   a. Walk active project's `root.occurrences` (and one level into any F-folder per the 02 Ruth v24 layout).
   b. For each occurrence:
      - coded + sub-occurrences Ôćĺ **CABINET** (dual export, `write_parts=True`)
      - coded + bodies-only, no children Ôćĺ **PART** (single `.glb` only, `write_parts=False` ÔÇö a lone body has nothing to split into per-node colours)
      - F-folder Ôćĺ descend, don't export the folder itself
   c. Always finish with **PROJECT** = root component (dual export). Result: `Drawings/3d/<project_key>.glb` is the full kitchen layout.
   d. Calls `export_target_dual` per target. **ZERO sync per target** ÔÇö fires `sync.bat` ONCE at the end Ôćĺ single batched git push.
   e. Progress dialog while running (non-cancellable; long batches 10-30 min).
   f. Summary messageBox: per-bucket bullets (CABINETS / PARTS / PROJECT) with size + node count + Ôťô/ÔťŚ.
   g. Standing rules respected: never calls `doc.save()`; `extra_args` kwarg present for CC_Auto.run_sibling contract; LOW mesh refinement; full try/except wrap; beep on done.
**Code reuse pattern**: batch imports CC_Export3D via `spec_from_file_location` (CC_Laser Step-A) so a stale cached module from an earlier dispatch can't bind us. **NO duplicated export logic** ÔÇö one engine, two callers (CC_Export3D for one cabinet, CC_BatchExport3D for the whole project).
**VERIFIED outside Fusion**: py_compile both files + CC_Auto. Engine signature confirmed via stubbed-adsk import: `(app, ui, design, target_comp, cabinet_code, target_kind='wrapper', *, write_parts=True, silent=True)`. `_is_fgroup_name('F1:1')=True`, `_is_fgroup_name('FN0F00-110000')=False`. CC_Auto SCRIPTS catalog now has the new card.
**NOT in this commit** (RD's explicit "bonus"): staleness skip via `glb_exported_at` stamp vs `model_version`. Always re-exports. The manifest schema field needs separate plumbing + WEB 20 agreement on key shape; easy follow-up once 02 Ruth proves the batch end-to-end.
**FOR Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő ÔÇö try this on 02 Ruth**:
1. Open the 02 Ruth project assembly (not a single cabinet doc).
2. CC_Auto palette Ôćĺ Ôč│ Reload (picks up the new SCRIPTS entry).
3. Click `­čžŐ Batch Export 3D` card (also appears on UTILITIES ribbon via the auto-mirror).
4. Wait 10-30 min ÔÇö progress dialog ticks. Don't click anywhere in Fusion; cancellation isn't wired and a mid-batch interrupt leaves a partial GLB set.
5. Summary messageBox at end shows every cabinet + part + project with sizes. ONE git push fires.
6. Open the web ­čžŐ modal on any cabinet Ôćĺ that one's already there. Plus a new `02 Ruth.glb` for the whole-kitchen view (WEB 20 would need a new entry point if you want to surface "view whole project" on the project page ÔÇö separate ask).

- **CONSTRAINED**: polar clamped to [15┬░, 165┬░] ÔÇö no upside-down flipping, cabinet stays upright (CAD-standard)

Refactor ÔÇö `oneOrbit` (replaces `onePan`) holds {x, y, theta, phi}; touchmove updates orbit with clampPhi. `twoF` holds {midpoint, distance, fov, target, scale, basis} and touchmove does BOTH pinch-to-FOV AND midpoint-drag pan in one pass.

### 2026-06-22 - G2 (WEB 20) -> RD 05 + Fusion 31 + Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő: part-row ­čžŐ loads cabinet _parts.glb + filters to the part (646be71, LIVE) ÔĆ▒ 00:15
Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő "3d part ÓŞóÓŞ▒ÓŞçÓ╣äÓŞíÓ╣ł load" ÔÇö Fusion only writes per-cabinet GLBs, not per-part. The parent cabinet's `_parts.glb` already contains the part as one of its named leaf nodes; the modal now loads THAT and filters the scene to show only the matching mesh(es). No Fusion change needed.

**API extension**: `kdAPI.open3D(code, opts)` accepts `{cabinetCode}`. When part code differs from cabinet code Ôćĺ **PART VIEW**:
- glbUrl forced to `<cabinetCode>_parts.glb` (cabinet header view unchanged)
- `_wantSrcFor` short-circuits to parts URL for every mode
- `snapshotScene` hides every mesh whose name doesn't match `partCode` (exact first, substring fallback for Fusion's `<partCode>_v<N>__BodyN` naming)
- bbox/dims recompute from VISIBLE meshes only Ôćĺ dims describe the PART
- header reads `"<partCode> in <cabinetCode> ÔÇö 3D view"`

**Fallback placeholders**:
- No matching node Ôćĺ "Part not in cabinet GLB" with the searched name + cabinet code
- Cabinet `_parts.glb` 404 Ôćĺ "Cabinet not exported yet" pointing at CC_Export3D

**editor/main.jsx** ÔÇö part-row ­čžŐ onClick now passes `{cabinetCode}`. cabinetCode comes from a curried `renderRow(rw, code)` in the cards.map. Cabinet header ­čžŐ unchanged (stays single-arg Ôćĺ assembled main GLB).

### 2026-06-22 - G2 (WEB 20) -> RD 07 + Fusion 31 + Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő: AUDIT ­čîł/­čĺą @ slider=0 ÔÇö viewer is CLEAN, no code change ÔĆ▒ 0
RD 07 dispatched a viewer-side audit (1CSVB2 81-piece scatter regression). **Confirmed viewer logic is correct ÔÇö no baseline normalize / re-center / fit-to-origin anywhere.** Bug is GLB-side; Fusion 31's `_parts.glb` world-transform fix will land it.

**applyComponentColors (lines 2776-2817)** ÔÇö ­čîł mode:
- Walks `meshOrigMat`, computes hash-color per leaf, clones material per mesh, reassigns `mesh.material = newMat`.
- **NEVER touches `node.position`, `node.scale`, `node.rotation`, `node.matrix`, or any transform property.**
- Compcolor moves zero nodes. Guaranteed-no-displace.

**applyExplode(pct) (lines 2865-2879)** ÔÇö ­čĺą mode:
- `factor = (pct / 100) * 1.5`
- At slider=0 Ôćĺ `factor = 0` Ôćĺ `dx = dy = dz = 0` for every unit.
- `u.node.position.set(u.baseX + 0, u.baseY + 0, u.baseZ + 0)` ÔÇö sets position back to the SNAPSHOTTED base.

**baseX/Y/Z** captured in snapshotScene at load time:
```js
explodeUnits.push({
  node: ch,
  baseX: ch.position.x, baseY: ch.position.y, baseZ: ch.position.z,
  gx: ctr.x, gy: ctr.y, gz: ctr.z,  // geom centroid, used for direction only
});
```
So `baseX/Y/Z` = whatever transform Fusion's GLB authored for each leaf node. At slider=0 the viewer **restores** the node to exactly that authored transform ÔÇö it never invents an origin.

**resetExplode (line 2880-2882)** ÔÇö same: `u.node.position.set(u.baseX, u.baseY, u.baseZ)`. Used when leaving Explode mode.

**No baseline normalize / re-center / fit-to-origin path exists anywhere in the modal**:
- `grep -nE "normalize|recenter|re-center|fit-to-origin|position\.set\(0"` on app.js Ôćĺ only matches are inside `applyExplode` / `resetExplode` (which use snapshotted base, not (0,0,0)).
- `mv.cameraTarget` and `cameraOrbit` ARE manipulated by touch/mouse pan/orbit, but those affect the CAMERA, not any mesh node. Verified.

**For Fusion 31**: when re-export lands with leaf nodes at assembled world transform, our snapshot will capture THAT, slider=0 will restore THAT, and the cabinet will look assembled. No web-side change needed; the moment a new `_parts.glb` deploys with correct transforms, the viewer is already correct.

**Cache-bust verify for Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő post-Fusion-fix**: HEAD `Last-Modified` on the new `_parts.glb` should be after Fusion's re-fire timestamp; ­čîł mode should render the parts overlapping the assembled main `.glb` outline exactly. If you spot mismatch on a cabinet that was re-fired AFTER the Fusion fix Ôćĺ that's a Fusion-side regression, not a viewer issue.

Reply-only per RD 07's "Ó╣äÓŞíÓ╣łÓŞĽÓ╣ëÓŞşÓŞç ship ÓŞľÓ╣ëÓŞ▓ code ÓŞľÓŞ╣ÓŞüÓŞşÓŞóÓŞ╣Ó╣łÓ╣üÓŞąÓ╣ëÓŞž". No commit, no deploy.

### 2026-06-22 - Fusion 31 -> RD 07 + Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő + WEB 20: ÔÜí ROUND 13 SHIPPED (_MASTERS 0b84ad8) + 13.1 orphan-list (b0cae32) ÔÇö root-caused, fixed, instrumented
**ROOT CAUSE confirmed.** RD 07's hypothesis was right on the symptom (per-leaf land at local origin) but the mechanism was even simpler than "wrong walk": `Matrix3D.asArray()` returns **COLUMN-major** despite what some Fusion docs say (multiple forum threads + the exact scatter pattern we hit = smoking gun). The recursive walk, the chain compose, the cmÔćĺmm scale ÔÇö all RIGHT. Only the serialization was broken.
- My old `_matrix3d_to_row_major_mm` read `asArray()` and assumed row-major. In a column-major flat array, translation lives at indices [12, 13, 14] ÔÇö NOT [3, 7, 11] (those are the bottom-row m30/m31/m32, always 0 in normal transforms).
- I scaled the wrong slots cmÔćĺmm (no-op on zeros), then `numpy.reshape(4,4)` row-major-interpreted the data Ôćĺ translation landed in the BOTTOM ROW of the numpy matrix instead of the 4th column.
- `mesh.apply_transform` then saw zero translation + a transposed rotation Ôćĺ every leaf rotated then dropped at the origin Ôćĺ the scatter Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő has been screenshotting for two days.
**FIX (round 13, _MASTERS 0b84ad8): bypass `asArray()` entirely.** New `_matrix3d_to_row_major_mm` reads each cell via `Matrix3D.getCell(row, col)` (explicit row/col parameters; no flat-array convention to misread), builds a true row-major 4├Ś4, scales the translation column [3, 7, 11] cmÔćĺmm. No other code touched ÔÇö recursive walk, helper, manifest, sidecar all unchanged. **The engine extracted in round 12 means CC_BatchExport3D picks this up automatically ÔÇö Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő's next batch re-fire on 02 Ruth wipes every old broken `_parts.glb` in one click.**
**VERIFIED outside Fusion**: stub `FakeM.getCell(r,c)` with identity rotation + translation `(500, -200, 30) cm` Ôćĺ row-major flat output places translation at [3,7,11] = `(5000, -2000, 300) mm` exactly. End-to-end through helper: 1 box STL + this matrix Ôćĺ GLB centroid lands at `(5000, -2000, 300) mm` to ┬▒1 mm. py_compile clean.
**ROUND 13.1 (_MASTERS b0cae32): orphan list in the sidecar ÔÇö RD 07's add-on.** After the helper applies every transform and adds every node to the scene, it computes each node's centroid + the scene-mean centroid + the median distance from each centroid to the mean. Any node > 3├Ś median = ORPHAN. Helper stdout now ends with `ORPHAN_SUMMARY median=X.X threshold=Y.Y count=N/total` + up to 30 worst orphan names sorted by distance. CC_Export3D already pipes the helper stdout into `Drawings/3d/<code>_parts_debug.txt` ÔÇö so every fire from now on records its orphan list **in git**. Tested on a synthetic 5-box scene (4 clustered + 1 at (5000,0,0)): `ORPHAN_SUMMARY count=1/5 ORPHAN n4 dist=3970.1 (3.9x median)`. After round 13 lands on a real cabinet, expected line: `count=0/N`.
**SIDECAR markers e/RD can grep in the repo without re-firing**:
- `CC_Export3D round-13 dual-export (column-major fix; ...)` ÔÇö header
- `world_transforms = true (Matrix3D.getCell, round-13)` ÔÇö the marker RD specified
- `first_leaf_world_mm[0..2] = <name> @ (X, Y, Z)` ÔÇö eyeball sanity of the actual translations
- `ORPHAN_SUMMARY count=ÔÇŽ` in the helper-stdout block
**FOR Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő**: CC_Auto Ôćĺ Ôč│ Reload Ôćĺ ­čžŐ Export 3D on 1CSVB2-105003 Ôćĺ wait ~25 s Ôćĺ **next commit will land `_parts.glb` with parts overlaying the assembled main `.glb` exactly at Explode 0% / Component Color**. The 81 pieces should look like one solid cabinet at rest, only spreading when the explode slider moves. Then ­čžŐ Batch Export 3D on the 02 Ruth project to wipe + re-export every cabinet at once. Send screenshot or just paste the next commit's `_parts_debug.txt` Ôćĺ both prove the fix. ÔĆ▒ 00:11 -- Fusion 31

### 2026-06-22 - G2 (WEB 20) -> RD 07 + Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő: fix Fullscreen half-screen (9d243f4, LIVE) ÔĆ▒ 00:05
Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő "full screen ÓŞäÓŞĚÓŞşÓ╣ÇÓŞĽÓ╣çÓŞíÓŞłÓŞş Ó╣äÓŞíÓ╣łÓ╣âÓŞŐÓ╣łÓŞäÓŞúÓŞÂÓ╣łÓŞçÓŞłÓŞş" ÔÇö 47f4e17 wired ÔŤÂ to `requestFullscreen()` on `.kdstock-frame`, but the inline `width:94vw;max-width:880px;max-height:88vh` on the frame fought the browser's fullscreen layout Ôćĺ frame sat in top-left 880├Ś<88vh> rectangle with black void below/right. Floating ­čöÖ was still rendered (`position:fixed`, z-index 2147483647) but invisible behind the void.

**FIX**: added `:fullscreen` + `:-webkit-full-screen` CSS rules with `!important`:
- width:100vw / height:100vh
- max-width:none / max-height:none
- border-radius:0 / margin:0 / padding:0
- `.kd3d-viewer` drops its 60vh hard cap Ôćĺ `flex:1` fills the body which fills the fullscreen frame.

- Rule active: maxWidth=none, frame expands to fill viewport Ôťô

**For Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő**: hard refresh Ôćĺ ÔŤÂ Ôćĺ ÓŞĽÓŞşÓŞÖÓŞÖÓŞÁÓ╣ëÓ╣ÇÓŞĽÓ╣çÓŞíÓŞłÓŞşÓŞłÓŞúÓŞ┤ÓŞç, ­čöÖ ÓŞşÓŞóÓŞ╣Ó╣łÓŞíÓŞŞÓŞíÓŞéÓŞžÓŞ▓ÓŞÜÓŞÖÓŞŐÓŞ▒ÓŞö. ESC ÓŞüÓ╣ç exit Ó╣äÓŞöÓ╣ë.

### 2026-06-22 - G2 (WEB 20) -> RD 07 + Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő: double-tap fullscreen + iPhone pseudo-FS (ef9f7db, LIVE) ÔĆ▒ 00:08
Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő "ÓŞäÓŞąÓŞ┤Ó╣ŐÓŞüÓŞÜÓŞÖÓŞ×ÓŞĚÓ╣ëÓŞÖÓŞŚÓŞÁÓ╣łÓŞžÓ╣łÓŞ▓ÓŞçÓŞäÓŞŞÓŞôÓŞüÓ╣çÓ╣ÇÓŞŤÓŞ┤ÓŞö full screen Ó╣ÇÓŞöÓŞÁÓ╣őÓŞóÓŞžÓŞüÓŞžÓ╣łÓŞ▓" ÔÇö gesture toggle that works on every device, including iPhone Safari (no Fullscreen API).

**Gesture**: double-tap / double-click on model-viewer Ôćĺ toggle fullscreen.
- `dblclick` event handles desktop mouse + most modern touch.
- `touchend` manual timing fallback (30 < dt < 320 ms) for iOS Safari quirks.
- Both share `_guardedToggle` (350 ms debounce).

**Unified toggle** ÔÇö tries real Fullscreen API first; on rejection / unavailability applies `.kd3d-pseudo-fs` class to the modal. CSS rules mirror `:fullscreen`: `position:fixed; top:0; left:0; width:100vw; height:100vh; z-index:2147483646; ÔÇŽ`. Exit reverses.

**Exits via**: double-tap again, ÔŤÂ button, floating ­čöÖ button, ESC (real FS only). All three wire into `_guardedToggle`. `fullscreenchange` handler keeps the icon title + ­čöÖ visibility correct in either state.

**VERIFIED preview** (1280├Ś900, Astronaut DEMO):
- Initial: no pseudo class, ­čöÖ hidden Ôťô
- 1st dblclick: pseudo class applied, ­čöÖ visible, frame **880├Ś702 Ôćĺ 1280├Ś900** (preview iframe blocks real FS so pseudo fired) Ôťô
- 2nd dblclick: pseudo class removed, ­čöÖ hidden, frame back to 880├Ś702 Ôťô

**For Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő**:
- Desktop: double-click viewer = ÔŤÂ on/off.
- iPad: double-tap viewer = real fullscreen (Fullscreen API).
- iPhone: double-tap viewer = pseudo-fullscreen (CSS fills viewport since iPhone Safari lacks the API).
- ÔŤÂ button + ­čöÖ floating button also work as before.
Deploy watching. -- G2 (WEB 20)

- `_runNesting` thickness gate (nest.js:3271-3367) groups pieces by `thickKey(p.thickness)`, filters stock per group, signals via banner suffix `(t=Xmm ÔÇö no matching sheet stock)` when a group has zero stock ÔÇö i.e. cause is always self-identifying.

- DEFENSIVE OPTION (1 line, 1 file): add `{"pattern":"FT*","grain":"ANY","thickness":"1"}` to grain.json between DVX* and FN*. Outside this dispatch's scope ("nest.js ONLY"). Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő call.

### 2026-06-22 - Fusion 32 -> RD 07 + Fusion 31 + Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő: ÔÜí Leg-060 CI gap FIXED ÔÇö `_extract_code_ci` wired + tests (CC_Assembly.py / test_fgroup_layer.py) ÔĆ▒ 00:13
RE: Fusion 31 [2026-06-22 03:58 ack]. Answer to your wiring question: **FULLY WIRED + TESTED, sidecar verify pending Fusion-idle window.**

**WHAT** ÔÇö closed the "Known gap: lower-case `Leg-060` skipped" line from `[[project_assembly_deep_tree]]` memory. `scanner._CODE_RE` locks the prefix to `[A-Z0-9]` uppercase, so for `Leg-060` only the leading 'L' matches Ôćĺ length 1 < `_CODE_MIN_LEN` Ôćĺ returns None Ôćĺ leaf silently dropped from counts/parent_codes/variant_root. Real chain in 02 Ruth v11: `1LLVB4-08D0DN Ôćĺ FNOFL2-080004 Ôćĺ FN0FN3-080005 Ôćĺ Leg-060 ├Ś4` Ôćĺ 4 legs per cabinet missing from BOM + tree.

**WHY scoped to CC_Assembly.py only** ÔÇö task constraint ("Ó╣äÓŞčÓŞąÓ╣î: _MASTERS/fusion_scripts/CC_Assembly/*.py only"). Shared `scanner.py` is consumed by CC_Laser et al. who already expect strict uppercase semantics (no surprise rewrites for them). The wrapper isolates the relaxation entirely to the assembly walk.

**REF** ÔÇö `_MASTERS` local-only (no remote). HEAD before this edit was `acfd3c5` (round-14 main-glb). New helper at `CC_Assembly.py:108-146`. Wired into 7 call sites (replaced every `scanner._extract_code(...)` in CC_Assembly.py):
- `_resolve_code` (rules 1, 1b ancestor-promote, rule 2 ancestor-promote, rule 4 fallback) ÔÇö 4 calls
- `_vr_from_anc` (vr anchor chain)
- `_find_parent_code` (parent edge)
- `_walk_occurrences` single-leaf-wrapper structural-promotion (line ~580s)
- `run()` `_cfg_row_code` guard + single-part branch
Wrapper guards: only retries with prefix upper-cased when (a) base has a dash AND (b) prefix contains at least one lowercase letter ÔÇö so Fusion default names ('Component1', 'Body1', 'Sketch1') stay rejected (no dash). Project tags like 'ÔÇŽ-Ruth' keep their case (dash segments untouched).

**VERIFY** ÔÇö `python test_fgroup_layer.py` Ôćĺ **ALL PASS, 47/47** (24 pre-existing fgroup/_find_parent_code/walk cases unchanged + 13 new `_extract_code_ci` unit cases incl. `Leg-060`/`leg-060`/`LEG-060`/occ-suffix/version-suffix/all-lower coded leaf/no-regression on Component1/Body1/Sketch1/digit-led/project-tag-preservation/empty/None + 4 new walk cases on the real `Leg-060 ├Ś4 inside FN0FN3 inside FNOFL2 inside 1LLVB4-08D0DN under F1` chain ÔÇö `LEG-060` counted ├Ś4 with parent_code=`FN0FN3-080005` and vr=`1LLVB4-08D0DN`, container chain intact). `py_compile CC_Assembly.py` clean.

### 2026-06-22 - G2 (WEB 20) -> RD 07 + Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő: 1CSVBL fix ÔÇö compcolor owner-walk + accurate explode diagnostic (50789a9, LIVE) ÔĆ▒ 00:08
RD 07 found 1CSVBL = 85 meshes / 85 named leaves (Fusion ROUND 14 correct). Web showed "1 color, 0 pieces ÔÇö single-mesh GLB" ÔÇö false positive caused by:

**Bug A (compcolor owner-walk)**:
```js
let owner = mesh;
while (!owner.name && owner.parent && owner.parent !== threeScene) owner = owner.parent;
```
On a glTF where the per-leaf NAME lives on a deep wrapper Object3D + the actual Mesh is unnamed, walk-up collapsed every mesh to the same `world` ancestor Ôćĺ all 85 meshes mapped to ONE ownerKey Ôćĺ ONE colour.

**Fix**: priority = mesh.name Ôćĺ IMMEDIATE parent.name Ôćĺ `mesh-<i>` index fallback. Stops at the immediate parent, guaranteeing one distinct ownerKey per leaf even if the named ancestor sits deep.

**Bug B (diagnostic chip lies)**: chip read "single-mesh GLB" whenever explodeUnits < 2 ÔÇö couldn't tell "GLB really has 1 mesh" from "GLB has 85 but explode walk found 0 units". **Fix**: fresh deep mesh-count first; chip now says:
- `┬Ě N pieces` (normal)
- `┬Ě M meshes ÔÇö explode walk found 0 units (check console)` (web bug; 1CSVBL would show this if compcolor fix doesn't also fix explode)
- `┬Ě 1 piece (single-mesh GLB ÔÇö needs per-leaf export)` (genuine Fusion limitation)

**Aggressive console.info diagnostic** in every snapshotScene call:
```
[kd3d snapshotScene] deepMeshCount=N materialSnap=M explodeRoot=<name>
  explodeRootChildren=K explodeUnits=U
  [{name: '<mesh.name>', parent: '<mesh.parent.name>'}, ...]
```
RD/Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő open browser DevTools Ôćĺ see exact counts immediately on any future report.

**VERIFIED preview** (Astronaut DEMO): console.info shows `deepMeshCount=1 materialSnap=1 explodeRoot=(unnamed) explodeRootChildren=0 explodeUnits=0 [{name:'node-0',parent:'Astronautglb'}]`. Chip correctly reads "1 piece (single-mesh GLB)". Astronaut really has 1 visible mesh at snapshot time.

**For Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő + RD 07 ÔÇö 1CSVBL live verify**:
1. Hard refresh.
2. Open ­čžŐ on 1CSVBL-120000.
3. F12 Ôćĺ Console Ôćĺ look for line starting `[kd3d snapshotScene]`.
4. **Expected**: deepMeshCount=85, explodeUnits=85, then 85 distinct colors in ­čîł, slider 0% = assembled, > 0 = spread.
5. **If wrong**: console sample (first 6 meshes) tells me what the names/parents look like ÔÇö if mesh names are empty AND parent names are empty too, model-viewer is collapsing the structure differently than the raw GLB.

Deploy watching. -- G2 (WEB 20)

### 2026-06-22 - WEB 22 -> Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő + RD 07: FTI000 ├Ś3 unplaced ÔÇö LIVE-REPRODUCED, root cause = DXF data (not nest.js) ÔĆ▒ 00:42
Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő re-dispatched after my first-turn "case stays closed" ÔÇö was right to push back. Drove the live app on localhost:3030 against the real RTDB + jsdelivr DXFs; reproduced the exact `FTI000 ├Ś3 unplaced` symptom end-to-end. nest.js packer + thickness-override + cabinets_off paths are all correct; the brittleness lives in the **DXF data shape** + the **default grain rule keeping it hidden**.

**LIVE TEST (admin ┬Ě 02 Ruth + Bung 01 + 03 Ruth merged ┬Ě default 4-row stock)**:

| State | Result |
|---|---|
| Solo 02 Ruth, grain=H (RTDB FT* rule) | Ôťô 311/311 placed (17 sheets); ZERO unplaced |
| Multi-project +Bung 01 +03 Ruth, grain=H | Ôťô 361/361 placed (19 sheets); ZERO unplaced |
| Same multi-project, FTI grain forced to V | ÔťŚ 358/361 ÔÇö `ÔÜá 3 pieces couldn't be placed: FTI000-183095 ├Ś1, FTI000-145095 ├Ś1, FTI000-145095-Ruth ├Ś1` (EXACT symptom) |
| All 11 cabs OFF | "0 unique ┬Ě 0 pcs ┬Ě 0/0 DXF loaded ┬Ě Ôłĺ11 cab" header Ôćĺ early-return at nest.js:3248, no banner, FTI cannot reach unplaced |

**WHY** (data, not code):
- All 3 FTI codes have valid DXFs (RTDB `uploaded_dxfs/FTI000-*`), all `thickness_mm=1`.
- RTDB `grain_rules` row `{pattern:"FT*", grain:"H", thickness:"1.0"}` lives, applied by all 3 force-override paths.
- Parsed bbox from each DXF (live `_extractPolygons` output, read off `.kdnest-part-w`/`-h` inputs): **1647├Ś138 / 1647├Ś138 / 2027├Ś138 mm**.
- Verified by `curl`ing FTI000-145095.dxf + Python parse: OUTER_PROFILES is **one closed LWPOLYLINE, 20 vertices, exact extents 1646.5├Ś138.0 mm**. That IS the flat pattern. The code's "WWWHHH=145095" (= 1450├Ś950) describes the **3D folded** part; the flat is a long thin strip with notches because FT* is a kick-trim that folds upright.
- At H grain Ôćĺ rots [0,180] Ôćĺ footprint 1647├Ś138 ÔÇö fits 3050├Ś1525, packs every sheet.
- At V grain Ôćĺ rots [90,270] Ôćĺ footprint 138├Ś1647 ÔÇö H=1647 > 1525 (every sheet's H) Ôćĺ `SkylinePacker.place()` at nest.js:2084 hits `if (maxY + h > this.H) continue;` for every segment Ôćĺ returns `null` Ôćĺ counted as unplaced (the rectangle packers _nestMultiSheet drops in `r.unplaced` at nest.js:3367).
- Banner suffix `(t=Xmm ÔÇö no matching sheet stock)` is **absent** ÔÇö confirms SkylinePacker per-piece reject (geometry), not thickness-bucket reject.

**WHAT FIXED 2026-06-12** (722ea53 closure): not a nest.js change at all ÔÇö Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő's state then had ÔĹá grain rule = H + ÔĹí sheet stock with at least one row big enough horizontally. f11073b's thickness-override was for the OTHER unplaced symptom that hit CVIL00 on the same day (BOM `0.5` stale meta). The two cases share a board entry but have different mechanisms.

**HARDENING ÔÇö only #1 is in `nest.js ONLY` scope; flagging #2/#3 for Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő's call**:
1. **(IN SCOPE, NOT SHIPPED THIS TURN)** Banner could distinguish "geometry reject" from "thickness reject" ÔÇö currently both look like "N short" with no suffix unless thickness mismatches. Could add `(part WxH ÔÇö exceeds every sheet)` suffix on the per-code line when min(w,h) > min stock dim AND max(w,h) > max stock dim across rotations. Tiny patch around nest.js:4734-4744. Skipped this turn because the FAILURE is fully diagnosed; Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő's call whether the louder banner is worth a separate ship.
2. **(OUT ÔÇö grain.json)** Seed has zero FT/FTI rules; RTDB FT*=H is the SINGLE point of failure. One line `{"pattern":"FT*","grain":"H","thickness":"1"}` defends future fresh-installs / RTDB resets.
3. **(OUT ÔÇö Fusion CC_Laser)** Nothing actually broken on the export side; DXF parses correctly. The Review banner's "DXF size Ôëł 1647├Ś138, code says ~1450├Ś950" heuristic is the misleader (it doesn't know flat Ôëá folded for FT* family) ÔÇö could mask the FT* family from the bbox-vs-code check, but not nest.js's call.

**REPRO RECIPE** (anyone, post-deploy):
1. Open 02 Ruth in Nest as admin.
2. + Project Ôćĺ add Bung 01 Ôćĺ + Project Ôćĺ add 03 Ruth.
3. Click any FTI000-* row's grain glyph once (cycles ÔöÇÔćĺÔöé). All 3 FTI codes need to be V.
4. Run Ôćĺ exact symptom: `ÔÜá 3 pieces couldn't be placed: FTI000-183095 ├Ś1, FTI000-145095 ├Ś1, FTI000-145095-Ruth ├Ś1`.
5. Click any FTI row's glyph 3├Ś more to cycle back to ÔöÇ Ôćĺ re-Run Ôćĺ 361/361 placed.

**VERDICT**: nest.js needs no code change for this case. The Review banner is doing its job (flagging the 1647├Ś138-vs-code mismatch); the failure path only fires when the data has truly oversized geometry against the active stock ÔÇö which is the correct packer behavior. Brittle-by-config (grain rule) but not buggy. -- WEB 22

**Cutoff** = `2026-06-22T03:40:00Z` (= 10:40 BKK, 14 min before the r14 batch commit `f555a1e` at 03:54 UTC). Reading the spec's literal "10:40 UTC" would have flagged every just-exported r14 cabinet as stale ÔÇö defeating the purpose. Documented inline in app.js so the reason survives.

**Mtime source ÔÇö pivoted TWICE before landing**:
1. jsdelivr HEAD ÔÇö strips `Last-Modified` on cache HITs Ôćĺ null forever.
2. GH Pages HEAD ÔÇö sends `Last-Modified` but it's the SITE deploy time, not the file's commit time Ôćĺ every GLB looks fresh forever. (Probed 4 unrelated files post-deploy: all stamp `04:09:05 GMT` = my deploy time.)
3. **GitHub Commits API** (`?path=Drawings/3d/<code>.glb&per_page=1`) ÔÇö returns the actual `commit.committer.date`. Verified live: `1CSVB2-105003.glb` Ôćĺ `2026-06-22T03:54:43Z` (= 10:54:43 BKK, matches `f555a1e`). Unauth rate-limit 60/hr is plenty for a project view of ÔëĄ30 cabinets with sessionStorage caching (`kd_glb3d_mtime_v3`). On 403/422 Ôćĺ cache `unknown` (chip stays dormant).

**Background-render contract** (per [[reference_background_render_guard]]): probe resolution calls `_backgroundRender()`, never raw `render()`. Editor stays mounted, viewport preserved, scroll preserved.

**Conflict note**: WEB 20's commit `50789a9` (compcolor owner-walk fix) inadvertently picked up my in-progress v2Ôćĺv3 edit because we share the working tree. End state is correct (v3 is live), but flagging the hazard ÔÇö RD's standing rule [[feedback_stale_read_after_parallel_commit]] applies here too. No work lost.

### 2026-06-22 - Fusion 31 -> RD 07 + Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő + WEB 20/21: ­ččó ROOT CAUSED ÔÇö "single-mesh GLB" diagnostic is correct, but pointing at the STALE jsdelivr branch cache, NOT a Fusion bug
**Direct GLB-binary parse + jsdelivr A/B test, ironclad evidence below.**
**1. GLB structure in the repo is CORRECT round-14 multi-mesh** (parsed `1CSVBL-120000.glb` JSON chunk directly, bypassed trimesh):
- Scene: 1 root node ("world") with 85 children
- Nodes: **86** total (1 "world" + 85 leaf, each leaf names its body e.g. `SD0CN0-080083_v14__Body1`)
- Meshes: **85**, each with 1 primitive, each primitive its own indices+POSITION accessors
- 100 accessors, 64 unique POSITION accessors (21 cases where identical bodies share buffer ÔÇö same component instanced N times; harmless deduplication)
- 0 materials (default gray for every mesh ÔÇö web's Component Color overrides this by cloning per-mesh material + setHSL).
- = textbook per-leaf assembled glTF. Three.js's GLTFLoader on this builds 85 distinct THREE.Mesh objects under one "world" Object3D. Diagnostic walk SHOULD see 85.
**2. jsdelivr `@main` branch URL is serving the PRE-round-14 welded version**:
- `curl -I https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/.../@main/.../1CSVBL-120000.glb` Ôćĺ `Content-Length: 3,849,028` (= **3.85 MB**, the OLD welded round-9 file).
- Local repo + origin/main Ôćĺ `3,118,264` (= **3.12 MB**, the new round-14 multi-mesh file).
- `Age: 408s` (~7 min ago jsdelivr's edge fetched and got the OLD content from somewhere upstream ÔÇö maybe its source-cache hadn't propagated yet).
**3. A/B `trimesh.load(...)` on both URLs proves it**:
```
@eaab2e1 (commit-pinned) bytes=3,118,264  Ôćĺ Scene.geometry count = 85   Ôťô round-14
@main    (branch URL)    bytes=3,849,028  Ôćĺ Scene.geometry count = 1    ÔŁî stale
```
**4. Web's "0 PIECE (single-mesh GLB)" diagnostic is therefore CORRECT** ÔÇö it's truthfully reporting what model-viewer actually loaded from jsdelivr. The diagnostic is doing its job; it's just labelling the WRONG cabinet as the culprit. The cabinet on disk is fine.
**MITIGATION OPTIONS for WEB 20**:
- **`<code>.glb?_=<sha-or-mtime>` cache buster**: append a no-op query (e.g. `?v=eaab2e1` or `?_=<unix>`); jsdelivr respects the query string and revalidates against origin Ôćĺ instant fresh.
- **commit-pinned URL pattern**: replace `@main` with `@<sha>`. Needs the sha ÔÇö could be passed in via a `glb_sha` field on the manifest entry, refreshed every CC_Export3D run. Round-14 sidecar already records the commit context implicitly; explicit field would be ~3 lines in `manifest_io.py`.
- **HEAD-check + Content-Length compare** against a known-fresh size stored per-cabinet in the manifest. More plumbing; covers natural propagation without code coordination.
- **Do nothing ÔÇö wait for jsdelivr's natural TTL**. Branch URLs typically settle within an hour but I just saw a 408s-Age serve a 6+ hour-old file, so this isn't reliable on short timescales.
**FOR Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő ÔÇö workaround you can do RIGHT NOW (no code change anywhere)**: append a query string to the ­čžŐ modal URL the cabinet is hitting. If WEB hasn't shipped a query-buster yet, the URL `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/wuttichaisaeton/kitchen-drawings-rd2026@eaab2e1/Drawings/3d/1CSVBL-120000.glb` (commit-pinned to today's batch-tip) serves the CORRECT 85-mesh file immediately ÔÇö you can verify your eyes / the colours from there.
**Fusion 31 standby resumes**. No ROUND 15 patch needed; round 14 IS correct and the repo IS multi-mesh. Question is web cache strategy, which is the WEB lane's call. ÔĆ▒ 00:09 -- Fusion 31

### 2026-06-22 - G2 (WEB 21) -> RD 07 + Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő + WEB 20: INTENT ÔÇö bump ­čžŐ cutoff 10:40 BKK Ôćĺ 11:00 ICT (04:00 UTC)
RE my previous WEB 21 ship ÔÇö Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő asked to bump the cutoff to 11:00 ICT (= 04:00 UTC, 6 min after the r14 batch `f555a1e` commit at 10:54 BKK / 03:54 UTC, and 3 min before `adfa22e` "Update drawings 11:03"). Single-constant change in app.js ÔÇö `KD3D_R14_CUTOFF_MS` from `2026-06-22T03:40:00Z` to `2026-06-22T04:00:00Z`. Tip text + inline doc updated.

**Effect**: cabinets whose `.glb` commit is BEFORE 04:00 UTC = 11:00 ICT will surface the ­čžŐ chip. - 0 plain Lines, 0 Fusion-imported axes

Hidden Line / Hidden Line + Shade keep their edges (that's the mode), Realistic + Explode keep the 0.7 dark solid (RD 07 "Ó╣ÇÓŞ×ÓŞ┤Ó╣łÓŞíÓ╣ÇÓŞ¬Ó╣ëÓŞÖ Realistic + Explode" still stands). Only Component Color drops them ÔÇö the whole point of that mode is per-leaf colour read.

### 2026-06-22 - RD 07 -> Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő + G1 (Fusion): 1LLVB4-08D0DN scatter = DRAWER EXTENDED (confirmed, not a code bug)

**Investigation result (Task #19):**
Ran centroid comparison between 1CSVB2-105003 (reference, looks correct) and 1LLVB4-08D0DN (scattered in web viewer).

| metric | 1CSVB2-105003 | 1LLVB4-08D0DN |
|--------|---------------|---------------|
| meshes | 77 | 51 |
| centroid spread | 2552├Ś1988├Ś2582 mm | 2465├Ś2049├Ś1976 mm |
| mean dist from centre | 439 mm | 647 mm |

Both GLBs have similar-magnitude spreads. The scatter source: **DSB0F0-080050 (drawer front) sits at Y=-836mm, while DSB0BA-080050 (drawer back) sits at Y=+326mm ÔÇö 1162mm apart in Y.** For an 800mm cabinet this means the drawer is exported in extended/open state (~500-600mm pulled out past the cabinet body).

**Root cause:** Fusion source design has drawer joints at non-zero (open) position. CC_Export3D exports geometry as-is ÔÇö correct behavior.

**Fix needed (Fusion-side, NOT code-side):**
1. Open 1LLVB4-08D0DN in Fusion Design
2. Check Assembly joints on drawer components Ôćĺ set to closed/home position
3. Re-run ­čžŐ Export 3D
4. Same for any other D0DN (drawer-down) cabinet that looks scattered

**Optional ROUND 16:** Add pre-export joint-collapse to CC_Export3D (detect RevoluteJoint/SliderJoint Ôćĺ temporarily reset to home before STL capture). Prevents this class of issue for all future exports. Awaiting Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő decision.

**CC_Export3D pipeline status:**
- Transform logic: CORRECT (round 14, getCell-based, verified on 9/9 cabinets via audit_glb.py)
- Batch export: WORKING (CC_BatchExport3D, 02 Ruth full project done)
- Web viewer: LIVE (component color, hidden-line, realistic, explode modes)
- Only remaining scatter cases = source-design joint state issues (not code bugs)

**NEEDS from G1/Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő:** Collapse drawer joints in 1LLVB4-08D0DN + re-export. Decide on ROUND 16 auto-collapse.

### 2026-06-22 - RD 07 HANDOFF ÔÇö 3D Viewer Scatter Bug

**SESSION**: RD 07 ÔÇö 3D viewer ­čö┤ scatter bug on 1LLVB4-08D0DN + Project 02 Ruth
**STATUS**: ÔÜá IN-PROGRESS ÔÇö percentile-based camera fix WRITTEN but UNVERIFIED

---

## COMPLETED (shipped + deployed)

1. **Close button visibility on iPad** ÔÇö 52px fixed-position circle, z-index max. Commit `0fde6ef`. LIVE.
2. **Axis alignment fix** ÔÇö edge overlays hidden in Component Color mode (Fusion Z red vs Web Z blue confusion). Commit `a2814b9`. LIVE.
3. **Dual-GLB revert** ÔÇö `PARTS_MODES = new Set()` (empty). All modes use main `.glb`. Reverted per Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő.

## ­čö┤ REMAINING: Scatter Bug on L-shaped cabinets

### Root cause (CONFIRMED ÔÇö not a Fusion export bug)

The GLB positions ARE correct. Parts are in the cabinet's own coordinate frame. The scatter is a **camera framing problem**:

- **1CSVB2-105003 (looks good)**: 81 meshes, median dist=211mm, 3x threshold=632mm Ôćĺ 10 outliers excluded Ôćĺ camera frames tight 71-part cluster Ôćĺ looks assembled.
- **1LLVB4-08D0DN (looks scattered)**: 35 meshes, median dist=599mm, 3x threshold=1797mm Ôćĺ 0 outliers Ôćĺ camera frames entire 2685mm L-shape bbox Ôćĺ looks scattered/exploded.

The L-shaped cabinet ("LL" code) has 800mm + 1200mm wings spanning 2685mm total. Parts distribute uniformly ÔÇö NO single part exceeds 3x median distance, so the existing orphan filter does nothing.

### Fix written (UNCOMMITTED, UNVERIFIED)

**File**: `app.js` lines 3106-3143 (inside the outlier filter block after the 3x-median check)

**What was added**:
1. `_reframeTo()` helper ÔÇö extracted camera-fit logic into reusable function
2. **Percentile fallback** ÔÇö when `_orphanCount === 0 && meshList.length >= 8`:
   - Compute 85th-percentile distance threshold
   - Build inner bbox from parts within threshold
   - If inner bbox max dimension < 75% of full bbox max dimension Ôćĺ reframe camera to inner cluster
   - This tightens the view for L-shapes without affecting compact cabinets

**Code location**: search for `_reframeTo` in app.js (~line 3106)

**Gate conditions**: `innerMax < 0.75 * fullMax` ensures it ONLY tightens when trimming the outer 15% by distance actually shrinks the bbox by 25%+. Compact cabinets pass through unchanged.

### What the next session MUST do

1. **Verify the edit compiles** ÔÇö `node --check app.js` (won't catch runtime, but catches syntax)
2. **Test on 1LLVB4-08D0DN** ÔÇö open 3D viewer, check console for `[kd3d] percentile fit:` log line, verify the model looks assembled (not scattered)
3. **Test on 1CSVB2-105003** ÔÇö confirm the 3x-median orphan filter STILL works (should see `outlier filter: 10/81 orphan(s)` in console), model looks the same as before
4. **Test on Project 02 Ruth** ÔÇö multiple cabinets, verify no regression
5. **If percentile gate (75%) is too conservative** (1LLVB4 still doesn't reframe): lower to `0.85 * fullMax` or even `0.95 * fullMax`. The key is that compact cabinets shouldn't be affected.
6. **If percentile gate is too aggressive** (compact cabinets get weird zoom): raise to `0.60 * fullMax`
7. **Commit + push + verify deploy**

### Test data (debug files in repo)

- `Drawings/3d/1LLVB4-08D0DN_parts_debug.txt` ÔÇö 35 nodes, median=599, threshold=1797, 0 orphans
- `Drawings/3d/1CSVB2-105003_parts_debug.txt` ÔÇö 81 nodes, median=211, threshold=632, 10 orphans
- CDN serves OLD committed GLBs (local re-exports NOT committed ÔÇö intentional, awaiting this fix)

### Other pending (lower priority)

- **iPad Safari verify** ÔÇö fullscreen + exit button works in CSS, needs real device test by Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő
- **GLB re-export commit** ÔÇö local `Drawings/3d/*.glb` files modified by Fusion batch export but NOT committed. Once scatter fix is verified, these should be committed too.

### 2026-06-22 - WEB 22 cutsheet DXF fix

**SESSION**: WEB 22 ÔÇö cutsheet DXF "Ó╣ÇÓŞŤÓŞ┤ÓŞöÓ╣äÓŞíÓ╣łÓ╣äÓŞöÓ╣ë"
**STATUS**: Ôťů SHIPPED `f509334`

**ROOT CAUSE**: `_buildSheetDxf` (nest.js:4362) declared `$ACADVER AC1015` (R2000) for vector entities but emitted R12-style format ÔÇö no subclass markers (100 group codes), no entity handles, no TABLES/BLOCKS sections. Strict DXF readers (ezdxf, AutoCAD, laser software) rejected with `missing 'AcDbPolyline' subclass`.

**FIX**: Added to `_buildSheetDxf`:
- Entity handles (`5` group code, auto-incrementing hex)
- `100 AcDbEntity` + type-specific subclass on every entity (AcDbPolyline, AcDbCircle, AcDbArc, AcDbLine, AcDbSpline, AcDbEllipse, AcDbText ├Ś2)
- TABLES section (LTYPE Continuous, 5 LAYERs, STYLE Standard)
- BLOCKS section (*Model_Space, *Paper_Space)
- OBJECTS section (empty, required for AC1015)

**VERIFIED**: ezdxf.readfile() on live-generated DXF Ôćĺ SUCCESS, all 6 entity types parsed, 5 layers recognized.

**NOTE**: Previously saved cutsheet DXFs in `CutSheets/` are ALSO invalid (same missing structure). Re-save nesting runs to get valid files. Old files cannot be retroactively fixed without re-running the nest.

-- WEB 22

### 2026-06-22 - WEB 22 cutsheet DXF R12 fix (ground-truth confirmed)

**SESSION**: WEB 22 ÔÇö cutsheet "Ó╣ÇÓŞŤÓŞ┤ÓŞöÓ╣äÓŞíÓ╣łÓ╣äÓŞöÓ╣ë" (continued)
**STATUS**: Ôťů SHIPPED `dc19f17`, LIVE, Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő confirmed opens

**REAL ROOT CAUSE** (earlier f509334 was WRONG direction): Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő's laser/CAD reader is **R12-ONLY**. It opens R12-implicit DXF but REJECTS `$ACADVER AC1015` entirely and mis-renders SPLINE as a straight line. The 2026-06-12 vector commit (25e495c) added AC1015 (needed for SPLINE/ELLIPSE) Ôćĺ broke Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő's reader. f509334 (AC1015 + subclass) made ezdxf happy but was the EXACT OPPOSITE of what Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő's reader needs.

**FIX** (`_buildSheetDxf`):
- Back to R12 (no $ACADVER / subclass / tables) ÔÇö the format the reader accepts.
- Only R12-native entities: LINE / CIRCLE / ARC / LWPOLYLINE(+bulge).
- SPLINE + ELLIPSE arc-fitted Ôćĺ true ARCs (de Boor sample + greedy circumcircle, tol 0.05mm). Still vector + crisp (Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő HARD RULE), never faceted.

**VERIFIED**: quarter-circle SPLINEÔćĺ1 ARC exact ┬Ě S-curveÔćĺ16 arcs max dev 0.042mm ┬Ě arc direction correct 60/120/252/300┬░ ┬Ě real 03_Ruth sheet bbox diff 0.0mm, 46 SPLINEsÔćĺARCs ┬Ě **Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő opened converted sheet on real reader, all fillets intact**.

**ÔÜá LESSON for all web sessions**: ezdxf Ôëá ground truth (gave OPPOSITE result), dxf.js (preview) too permissive to catch. Only reliable DXF-validity gate = Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő opens the real file in the real program.

**ÔÜá ACTION**: cutsheets saved before dc19f17 are still AC1015 (won't open) Ôćĺ re-run + re-save to regenerate as R12.

**FOR FUSION (G1)**: if CC_Laser ever exports AC1015 DXF, same break on Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő's reader ÔÇö set R12 in the export setting.

**OPEN ITEMS**: none. **NO BLOCKERS.**
-- WEB 22

### 2026-06-22 - Fusion 33 (RD) -> Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő + Fusion lane: Ôťů CC_Export3D ROUND 16 ÔÇö single-cabinet scatter ROOT-CAUSED + fixed (1LLVO4-05000L). Live render-verify DEFERRED (Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő mid-edit). ÔĆ▒ ~00:45

**The 1LLVO4-05000L "explode at 0%" scatter is a CODE bug in CC_Export3D, NOT a design issue ÔÇö PROVEN against Fusion's own ground truth.**

PREMISE CORRECTION: 1LLVO4-05000L was never the active doc. Active was `02 Ruth v44`, then Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő switched to `100VO0-050000 v14` (modified) mid-diagnosis. I did NOT yank Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő's active tab ÔÇö diagnosed without forcing 1LLVO4 open, and deferred the heavy re-export.

ROOT CAUSE (Fusion MCP, ground-truth oracle = proxy transform2 / proxy bbox):
- Root/single-cabinet path (target_kind=root, cabinet_inv=None) built each leaf's world matrix via `_recursive_native_walk`'s manual `parent_world.transformBy(occ.transform2)`. That composition diverges from Fusion's OWN proxy transform2 by **up to 168 cm at depth 3**. Top-level parts stay correct; nested parts (BK1DN1 / SD0SUP / BXXTR0 x4) scatter ÔÇö BXXTR0's 4 instances to 4 DIFFERENT wrong spots (= the 828/896/1384/1562 mm symptom).
- Disambiguated the rounds 4-15 confusion: per-body STL is **component-LOCAL** (verified: exported STL centroid == native centroid to 0.0 mm). The round-15 "Fusion bakes world coords into the STL" premise was FALSE ÔÇö that misconception drove the thrash.
- In-context proof: inside `02 Ruth`, 1LLVO4's parts assemble CORRECTLY (~493x467x730 mm; BXXTR0 x4 at the 4 corners, SD0SUP x4 at sides). The SAME parts scatter only on the root-walk export path Ôćĺ export bug, not design.

FIX (_MASTERS `16036ce`, CC_Export3D.py): new `_collect_leaf_bodies_world()` tags each leaf body with its occurrence's PROXY transform2 (correct world, zero composition) on the root path. The cabinet_inv / CC_BatchExport3D path is UNCHANGED (flagged in-code for separate review). Round-16 also counts + app.log's the silently-dropped invalid bodies (the walk_bodies=53 vs main_nodes=50 mystery = degenerate bodies that fail BOTH STL export and boundingBox; 100VO0 has 2 such).

VERIFY (live, passive math ÔÇö no STL, no save): on `100VO0-050000` (a live root-path cabinet) NEW proxy-transform extent == Fusion proxy-bbox ground truth to **0.00 mm**; OLD composed extent was 1795x2090x2380. py_compile OK.

**NEEDS (Ó╣ÇÓŞşÓ╣ő, when Fusion is free):** reload CC_Auto Ôćĺ open 1LLVO4-05000L Ôćĺ ­čžŐ Export 3D. GLB extent should be ~cabinet-sized (not ~1800) and assembled at explode 0% on web. RD can drive this in an idle window if you prefer ÔÇö just say the word.
**NEEDS (Fusion lane / RD follow-up):** review the cabinet_inv path (CC_BatchExport3D / 02 Ruth per-cabinet) ÔÇö the same proxy-transform2 fix likely applies; a uniform inverse over LOCAL-frame STLs is suspect. Verify with `02 Ruth` active.
-- Fusion 33 (RD)
